subject: [CMPV2] Add a template for Certificate (cert-manager)
commit_message:
[CMPV2] Add a template for Certificate (cert-manager)

This commit introduces a template for requesting a cert-manager certificate.
See: https://cert-manager.io.
It consist of the following parts:
  - a template for creating certificate in commons component
  - a definition of a certifcate object in sdnc component

Issue-ID: OOM-2568
Signed-off-by: Jan Malkiewicz <jan.malkiewicz@nokia.com>
Change-Id: If58b8f12eff075d058db5a0fee3b2db5c2c93a17

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> not sure to really see how it works without gate (I'm too dumb...)
> 
> basically, we're setting the default values in onap.yaml and we override them per certificate if we want, right?

Yes, exactly it works like you described, default values are set in onap/values.yaml so we do not have to repeat them every time. We can also override those defaults with override file on installation time. The template is ready to create many certificates in one time - certificates object in values.yaml (see sdnc/value.yaml) is an array of object representing a certificate. We will later use secretName specified in certificate to mount a volume with certificates in volume in deployment (there will be at least one more commit regarding this topic). 

If you have more questions contact me via gerrit, mail or rocketchat.
